title: Drag-to-dismiss modal
design_intent: Design intent for anim-061: make Drag-to-dismiss modal legible as a framer motion state change, preserve focus and layout stability, and communicate the same meaning when reduced motion removes animation.
reference_inspiration: Reference for anim-061: iOS sheets; apply it narrowly to Drag-to-dismiss modal's timing, easing, and affordance, not as decorative motion.
motion_reasoning: anim-061 uses framer motion on web for Drag-to-dismiss modal. The motion must express Drag modal down to dismiss — natural gesture., survive interruption, preserve focus order, honor reduced motion, and keep animated work on transform or opacity where possible.

performance_strategy.thread_management: Keep gesture/motion state on the animation thread for Drag-to-dismiss modal; React state updates only at committed milestones.
performance_strategy.gpu_considerations: Animate only transform/opacity for Drag-to-dismiss modal; avoid layout-affecting properties and validate no long paint blocks.
performance_strategy.interrupt_handling: Interrupt by cancelling the active timeline, preserving focus, and snapping to the nearest semantic state for Drag-to-dismiss modal.

accessibility.reduced_motion: Respect prefers-reduced-motion, focus order, and interruption behavior for Drag-to-dismiss modal.
accessibility.screen_reader: Respect prefers-reduced-motion, focus order, and interruption behavior for Drag-to-dismiss modal.
accessibility.vestibular_safety: Respect prefers-reduced-motion, focus order, and interruption behavior for Drag-to-dismiss modal.

solution: <motion.div drag="y" dragConstraints={{top: 0, bottom: 300}} onDragEnd={(_, info) => { if (info.offset.y > 150 || info.velocity.y > 500) onClose(); }}/>
interaction_details: Drag down: dismiss
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive animation for Drag-to-dismiss modal only checks the first visual impression. It breaks when framer motion on web is interrupted, focus changes mid-transition, reduced-motion users need an instant equivalent, or layout and paint work exceed the frame budget.

solution:
const x = useSharedValue(0);
const pan = Gesture.Pan().onUpdate(e => { x.value = Math.max(-100, Math.min(0, e.translationX)); }).onEnd(e => { x.value = withSpring(x.value < -50 ? -80 : 0); });
interaction_details: Swipe: reveal delete
Tap delete: confirms
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive animation for List item swipe-to-delete only checks the first visual impression. It breaks when reanimated on mobile is interrupted, focus changes mid-transition, reduced-motion users need an instant equivalent, or layout and paint work exceed the frame budget.
